title: Race-specific expected-life adjustment
summary: Adjust generic compound-life windows with causal race-level tyre-age context rather than full-race future summaries.
rationale: Fixed SOFT/MEDIUM/HARD life constants were useful, but tyre longevity varies by circuit and race conditions. A causal race-specific adjustment may capture that variability without using future stint endpoints.
implementation_hint: For each race and compound, compute prior-lap or current-lap peer tyre-age baselines and smooth them toward global compound constants. Add adjusted current-tyre finish margins and old-for-race-compound flags using only current or earlier race context.
expected_effect: Small to medium ROC AUC gain with low-medium confidence.
risk: Race-specific baselines can be noisy early in a race; use conservative fallback constants and missingness flags.
